Ensure the preparation of complete and separate financial statements for each special account, such as Statement of Financial Position (Balance Sheet), Statement of Financial Performance (Statement of Income and Expenses), and Statement of Cash Flows and attach these to the General Fund trial balance as part of the year-end financial reporting requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

We recommend that Management:

Strictly enforce compliance with documentary requirements as mandated under Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001, and other applicable auditing and procurement regulations, to ensure the legality and propriety of disbursements; and
Strengthen internal control measure to ensure that no disbursement is processed or approved without complete and proper supporting documents.

Direct the Local Development Council to adequately plan and formulate a more responsive Annual Investment Plan incorporating specific PPAs aimed to promote socio-economic developments in the Municipality; and

We recommended that Management:
Discontinue the charging of non-disaster-related expenditures, including general administrative expenses and expenditures solely for social and health-related services, to the LDRRMF. These expenses should be properly charged to their appropriate accounts under the General Fund; and
Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to properly plan, identify, and prioritize specific programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) that are clearly aligned with the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.
Management Comment:
The municipal officials concerned justified that financial assistance for recovery and rehabilitation was granted to senior citizens who were duly affected by the drought. Meanwhile, the food and transportation allowance were provided to BPATs and Tanods in connection with their participation in training and orientation activities.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
While the justification of the Municipal Officials was duly noted, the granting of financial assistance to senior citizens, although affected by the drought, must still comply with the provisions of the JMC. The Recovery and Rehabilitation component of the LDRRMF is intended to support activities that are directly linked to restoring facilities, services, and livelihoods after the occurrence of a disaster, supported by proper needs assessments and documentation.
Moreover, the singling out of a specific sector, in this case, senior citizens, as the sole recipients of recovery assistance, without a documented needs assessment or clear selection criteria, may be viewed as selective and not reflective of an inclusive disaster response framework. The absence of clear criteria, eligibility requirements of the duly validated beneficiary lists, and approved rehabilitation plans weakens the justification for such disbursements. Accordingly, this can be more appropriately categorized under the LGU's regular social welfare programs rather than as a disaster-related intervention funded through the LDRRMF.
On the other hand, we recognize that the provision of transportation and food allowances to BPATs and Tanods during training activities may be considered allowable charges under the LDRRMF, provided that such activities are part of the duly approved DRRM Program. However, it was noted that the allowances were disbursed in December 2024, which does not coincide with the actual conduct of the training held in July. This discrepancy raises concerns regarding the appropriateness of the disbursement and suggests that the expenditure may not be properly aligned with the intended purpose and objectives of the DRRM program.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the OIC- Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices to submit the missing supporting documents or correct the deficiencies in the submitted records to establish the validity and propriety of the disbursements;
Strictly enforce compliance with documentary requirements as mandated under Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001, and other applicable auditing and procurement regulations, to ensure the legality and propriety of disbursements; and
Strengthen internal control measure to ensure that no disbursement is processed or approved without complete and proper supporting documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit a written justification for the charging of Rehabilitation and Recovery Financial Assistance to Senior Citizens against the LDRRMF and Special Trust Fund, including the basis for considering such assistance as within the scope of disaster-related programs, projects, and activities; and
Ensure that subsequent disbursements charged against the LDRRMF are strictly in accordance with the approved LDRRMFIP and AIP, and that any revisions thereto are properly justified, approved by the Local Sanggunian and the Local Chief Executive upon recommendation of the LDRRMC.

Require the OIC – Municipal Accountant and other accountable officers to strictly comply with the provisions of EO No. 77 in processing and approving claims for travel expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:

   a. Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to  maintain  property
      cards following the prescribed procedures in  property  recording  and
      maintain an updated, accurate and complete property records;


   b. Direct the GSO-Designate to submit an updated RPCPPE  as  of  December
      31, 2021;


   c.  Assign  a  full  time  Supply/Property  Officer,  if  possible,   and
      reconstitute the Inventory Committee and  prepare  Physical  Inventory
      Plan (PIP) specifying the  specific  assignments,  the  cut-off  date,
      specifying  the  schedule  dates  and  location  of  inventory  taking
      activities from start to the targeted date of completion  as  provided
      in section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;

   d. Direct the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  GSO-Designate  to  conduct
      reconciliation of PPE by  category  and  its  PPELC  and  PC  records,
      respectively and prepare adjustment in the books of accounts  and  the
      property records, respectively; and

   e. Require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant  to  look
      into and comply with the guidelines and procedures set  forth  in  the
      COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address issues and concerns on  the  non-
      existing and unserviceable assets.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
   a. Direct the Accountant and the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  (BAC)  to
      immediately submit all the lacking supporting documents enumerated  in
      Appendix I to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the  procurements  and
      strictly adhere to subsections of Section 9 of COA Circular No.  2012-
      001 and Appendix A of Annex H of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184;


   b. Direct the BAC  Secretariat  to  submit  contracts  and  its  attached
      documents to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days in
      adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001  and  Section
      9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.


   c. Direct  the  Accountant  to  ensure  proper  and  complete  supporting
      documents are attached to all disbursement vouchers;

   d. Direct the GSO-Designate to ensure the filling up of complete  data  in
      the POs/contracts indicating the  date  of  delivery  and  receipt  by
      supplier/contractor  signifying  his  conformity  to  the  terms   and
      conditions of the POs/contracts; and


   e. Direct the Acceptance and Inspection  Committee  to  indicate  in  the
      Acceptance and Inspection Report the actual  date  of  acceptance  and
      inspection of deliveries.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer to  prepare  and
      submit each month a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to  the
      audit team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and

   b. Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY  2021  in
      required format without delay.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

   a. Direct the GSO-Designate and the Accountant to maintain a  repair  and
      maintenance record/history  of  each  equipment/vehicle  owned  and/or
      operated by the Municipality to track down the frequency of repair and

      maintenance history/record to the disbursement  vouchers  for  repairs
      and maintenance expenses as supporting documents; and

      repairs can add up the life of the motor vehicles  and  recompute  the
      depreciation expense to the remaining life of the asset.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of “window dressing” and report collections on the period they were collected. It is also recommended that all collections be deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to send final demand letters requiring active officials and employees to immediately settle unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold the corresponding amount from their salary in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to evaluate long outstanding cash advances granted to transferred, retired, no longer in service and deceased employees, and process a request for an authority to write-off accounts, if applicable, in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant implement the one-time cleansing of PPE, record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, and submit the required reports to the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to close all completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts in accordance with existing laws and regulations.
We further recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant all required reports and documents upon completion of projects under the CIP account for appropriate recording in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Memorandum of Agreements or coordinate with the source agency to determine the correct disposition of each fund transfer, otherwise revert the unutilized balances to the source agency in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant investigate the cause of the abnormal negative balances in the SL of the Due to LGUs and NGAs, and record necessary adjustments in the books to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Health Officer furnish the Municipal Accountant with a detailed list of allocated and donated vaccines received by the Municipality for recording in the books of accounts;
For donations received with stated costs, the Municipal Accountant should record in the books the amount received in accordance with existing rules and regulations; and
For donations received without stated costs, the Municipal Accountant should recognize the donations at fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the receipt of goods and supplies as inventory in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
We further recommended that the Officer in charge of inventory, issue an RIS upon the release of supplies, and prepare the monthly RSMI which will be submitted to the Municipal Accountant as basis for drawing a JEV to record the issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator utilize 20% DF on projects that partake in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in strict compliance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer maximize the use of LDRRMF to provide the necessary "rescue and response equipment", and to operationalize the provisions of the National DRRM Plan and National Climate Change Action Plan.

We recommended that Management: Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to immediately submit the aforementioned supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the procurements of the ₱5.594 million; Direct the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, Item 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 particularly in the conduct of emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that Management: Strictly implement in a timely manner locally funded PPAs identified to contain the spread of COVID-19 and to provide basic services to the affected population; Maximize the utilization of all projects, programs and activities of its mandatory funds in relation to its Annual Budget and strictly implement in the ensuing year PPAs classified as continuing projects; Include only those PPAs which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership; Closely monitor and supervise PPA implementation so that problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed; and Mobilize necessary resources to undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate disaster response aid and measures in a safe and timely manner to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

We recommended that Management: Instruct the property custodian to maintain property cards following the prescribed procedures in property recording and maintain accurate and complete property records; Assign additional personnel for the inventory committee, if needed; Conduct annual reconciliation of PPE between the Municipal Accountant Office and the General Services Office records and cause adjustment in the books of accounts and the property records respectively, as warranted, after reconciliation; and Continuously reconcile the result of the physical count with the property and accounting records.

We recommended that Management: Consider feasibility of providing computer software/system to facilitate the completion of database of real property units and for more efficient and effective administration of the real property tax; Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant;

We recommended that Management: Facilitate the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU thereof pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386; Direct the Municipal Assessor to appraise the untitled lots to its current market value and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant for reference in recording in the book of accounts; Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the subject property assets upon securing the Certificate of Title using the actual costs or appraised value if the former could not be determined; and Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property assets with the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management: Strictly implement all projects, programs and activities of its mandatory funds in relation to its Annual Budget; See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise during the implementation shall be immediately addressed.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:


 a.  Consider  feasibility  of  providing   computer   software/system   to
    facilitate the completion of database of real property  units  and  for
    more efficient and effective administration of the real property tax;

    list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due  and  collectible
    for the year to the Municipal Accountant;

    beginning of the year and make necessary adjusting entries  to  reflect
    the correct income for the reporting period; and




 d.  Adopt  the  Accrual  Method  of  accounting  real  property  taxes  in
    accordance with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS  and  Section  246  of  the  Local
    Government Code of 1991.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

 a. Facilitate the processing of Certificate of  Title  of  the  untitled
    lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest
    of the LGU thereof pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;

    current market value and provide the same to the  Municipal  Accountant
    for reference in recording in the book of accounts;

    upon securing the Certificate  of  Title  using  the  actual  costs  or
    appraised value if the former could not be determined; and


 d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise  the  proper
    payments of expenses for the land titling and to update  and  reconcile
    the inventory of property  assets  with  the  updated  records  of  the
    Municipal Accountant.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:


 a. Strictly  implement  all  projects,  programs  and  activities  of  its
    mandatory funds in relation to its Annual Budget;

 b. Require the LDRRM Officer  that  during  identification  of  PPA’s  for
    budgeting, include only those which are feasible and can  be  completed
    within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the
    priorities of the LGU leadership; and

 c. See to it  that  PPAs  being  implemented  are  closely  monitored  and
    supervised  so  that  problems/issues  that  may   arise   during   the
    implementation shall be immediately addressed.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Initiate the compilation of information for all items of PPE (current and prior years)
at cost or fair value, duly supported with Construction in Progress Ledger Card
(CIPLC) through the Offices of LGU’s Engineer and Accountant;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify CIP account to appropriate asset
account, as soon as the construction is completed including all expenses during
the construction period;
c. Henceforth, observe accuracy and timely recording and maintenance of CIPLC
containing all on-going and uncompleted projects for proper monitoring;
d. Prepare the specific estimated useful life for each asset based on LGU’s
experience on the life of its PPE, copy furnished the Audit Team; and
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements regarding the status of any item in the PPE funded by a
loan.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendations that Management direct
the Heads of Property and Accounting Offices to reconcile the recorded
accountability of PPE compared with the existing PPE at reasonable intervals, and
appropriate action shall be taken with respect to any difference.
Moreover, direct the Property Officer Designate to properly accomplished the
RPCPPE and furnish the following offices:
a. Original copy - Accounting Unit
b. Duplicate copy - COA Auditor
c. Triplicate copy - Supply and Property Unit
d. Quadruplicate copy - Inventory Committee file

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Consider the 20% debt service ceiling and the net available funds prior to
loans/borrowings contracting pursuant to DOF LFC No. 1-2012;
b. Consider the type/nature and economic or useful life of the properties to be
financed and the remaining term of the contracting incumbent official in
determining the loan/borrowing terms;
c. Maintain updated subsidiary ledgers for each loan/borrowings contracted for
proper monitoring of the correct balances of the accounts and propriety of
expenses charged thereon; and
d. Consider a competitive rental fees for the Public Market as an additional source
of income to cover the annual loan amortization.

Thus, in order to establish a complete inventory and appraisal of all real properties
within LGU’s jurisdiction, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Assessor to establish proper coordination and linkages with
other heads of departments i.e., Treasury and Accounting or with other Local and
National Government Agencies i.e., Province, Register of Deeds and DAR
among others, to come up with an Assessment Roll (AR) and submit the AR to
the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of RPT Account Register/Taxpayer’s
index card;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly
certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
for the year; and
c. Based on the list, direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to record the entries debiting RPT/SET Receivables and crediting
Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET Income at the beginning of the year.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the BAC secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with perfected contracts
awarded for the period June 2015 to July 2016 as basis of computing the subject
honoraria, pursuant to DBM BC 2004-5 and 5A;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that succeeding payment of honoraria
are duly supported with complete attachments required for all completed projects
or contract;
c. Direct the concerned official to submit all completed contracts within the five (5)
working days from its execution pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 dated
February 12,2009; and
d. Require the BAC Secretariat to maintain a log book containing all relevant
information on the procurement process up to the award of contract so as to
provide bases for granting honorarium and overtime to personnel involve in
procurement activities.

We recommended that Management:
a. Take appropriate action to remit immediately the unutilized balance of
₱58,941.00 to the BOT, in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision (EN
BANC) dated November 19, 2013; and
b. Submit the Journal and Adjusting entries duly supported with remittance
advice/receipt.

e. For Non-submission of copies of contracts and purchase orders with supporting

 Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and Secretary to furnish the

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a) Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, wages, allowances, etc. by opening
of individual ATM savings accounts through a Memorandum of Agreement
(MOA) with any of the government servicing bank stationed in Roxas City; and

premises of the LGU.

Conduct the immediate count of property, plant and equipment and submit the RPCPPE using the format shown in Appendix 49 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU’s, Volume II; and Reconcile the balances shown in the RPCPPE with the balances per accounting records and prepare the necessary adjustments, if any, for the difference.
